mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during rate hikes with a t-test p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recessionary expectations that suppress corporate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate robust consumer demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a monthly increase in the industrial production index signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases produce negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and higher aggregate consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns with a rank IC below negative 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 600 thousand units, construction sector contraction and reduced household wealth formation trigger a downturn in consumer durables spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST values below 600.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 65 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, real interest rates decline and central bank tightening expectations accelerate, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL with flat FEDFUNDS yields positive forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansion phases, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms sustained demand recovery, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5 percent in early cycles is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of quarters.
